name: Iznatoraf - climbing loop via Villanueva del Arzobispo
name_original: Iznatoraf - bucle de subida por Villanueva del Arzobispo
region_intro: Road cycling around Iznatoraf is characterized by its elevated position at 1,036 meters (3,399 feet) above sea level, offering panoramic views and challenging ascents. The landscape is dominated by extensive olive groves, providing scenic backdrops for rides. The region's proximity to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Natural Park offers diverse terrain, from vast pine forests to rugged areas, making it ideal for varied road cycling routes. Best road cycling routes around Iznatoraf The most popular road cycling route is Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo, a 51.8 miles (83.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the varied terrain around the Tranco de Beas Reservoir, offering significant climbs and rewarding descents. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villacarrillo, Castellar, and Iznatoraf Loop, a difficult 37.8 miles (60.8 km) path. This route connects several local towns, traversing olive groves and offering views of the surrounding hills. Local road cyclists also love the Iznatoraf - climbing loop via Villanueva del Arzobispo, a 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail leading through olive groves and hilly terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Road cycling around Iznatoraf is defined by extensive olive groves, mountainous backdrops, and access to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to challenging ascents. The routes in Iznatoraf are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Iznatoraf's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Iznatoraf?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Iznatoraf, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate climbs to challenging ascents through the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Iznatoraf?
A: Road cycling around Iznatoraf is characterized by its elevated position, offering panoramic views and significant climbs and descents. The landscape is dominated by extensive olive groves, providing a scenic backdrop. You'll also find routes that venture into the diverse terrain of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Natural Park, featuring vast pine forests and rugged areas.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of road cycling in Iznatoraf?
A: The road cycling routes around Iznatoraf cater to various skill levels. While there are no easy routes listed, you'll find 15 moderate routes and 25 difficult routes, ensuring options for both intermediate and experienced cyclists looking for a challenge.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the routes in Iznatoraf?
A: The road cycling routes in Iznatoraf are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging topography, the stunning views over olive groves, and the rewarding descents.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Iznatoraf?
A: Given Iznatoraf's elevated position and the warm Andalusian climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, especially for challenging climbs, while winter might bring cooler temperatures but often still offers clear, sunny days.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Iznatoraf?
A: Yes, Iznatoraf's elevated position naturally offers many spectacular vistas. You can find viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Cabeza de la Viña Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides views near the Tranco reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabueno Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes traverse hillsides offering expansive views of the olive groves and the Sierra de Las Villas.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring historical sites in Iznatoraf?
A: Absolutely. Iznatoraf is a designated Historic and Artistic Site. You can explore its historic town center with narrow, labyrinthine streets, remnants of ancient walls and arches like the Arco de la Virgen del Postigo, and visit the Renaissance-style Parish Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción. These cultural points of interest can be a pleasant addition to your cycling itinerary.

Q: Are there any natural attractions to see while cycling?
A: Many routes offer glimpses of natural beauty. The region is close to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Natural Park, one of Spain's largest protected areas. You might pass by natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; or cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured in routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009289917/tranco-de-beas-reservoir-loop-from-villanueva-del-arzobispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Iznatoraf?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009305263/villacarrillo-castellar-and-iznatoraf-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacarrillo, Castellar, and Iznatoraf Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 60.8 km path that connects several local towns. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009289917/tranco-de-beas-reservoir-loop-from-villanueva-del-arzobispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 83.4 km route.

Q: Are there options for less experienced road cyclists?
A: While many routes are classified as difficult, there are moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009295856/iznatoraf-climbing-loop-via-villanueva-del-arzobispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznatoraf - climbing loop via Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.3 km trail that still offers a good climbing experience through olive groves and hilly terrain.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling climbs in the area?
A: Iznatoraf's elevated position ensures plenty of challenging climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009289917/tranco-de-beas-reservoir-loop-from-villanueva-del-arzobispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, with over 1,300 meters of climbing. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009295856/iznatoraf-climbing-loop-via-villanueva-del-arzobispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznatoraf - climbing loop via Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt; also offers a substantial climb over a shorter distance.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route in Iznatoraf?
A: As a historic town, Iznatoraf has limited parking within its narrow, ancient streets. It's advisable to look for parking options on the outskirts of the town or in designated areas before starting your ride. Larger nearby towns like Villanueva del Arzobispo or Villacarrillo, which are often starting points for routes, may offer more readily available parking facilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling trails near Iznatoraf?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite – Tranco de Beas Reservoir loop from Antigua Estacion de tren&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial challenge, covering over 146 km with significant elevation changes. This allows cyclists to explore a larger portion of the natural park and surrounding areas.
tips: Hilltop village with magical narrow streets and whitewashed buildings. The old town was once surrounded by walls and a small fort. The views over the olive groves of the surrounding landscape are spectacular. Well worth the climb.
The town of Villanueva del Arzobispo is located in the Sierra de las Cuatro Villas, surrounded by olive groves and cereal fields.
At 1.85km, this climb to the hilltop fortress city of Iznatoraf is short but tough, with an average grade of 9.1%. Great effort for training repetitions. Don't forget to check out the amazing views.
